article.title: Uber | SSE | Screening Round | Reject
article.content:
Inputs:

| O | E | E | E | X |
| E | O | X | X | X |
| E | E | E | E | E |
| X | E | O | E | E |
| X | E | X | E | X |

Location Map: A 2D grid where:

'O' represents a robot
'E' represents an empty space
'X' represents a blocker
Query Array: [2, 2, 4, 1]

These values represent the exact distances a robot must be able to move before hitting a blocker:
Left: 2 spaces
Top: 2 spaces
Bottom: 4 spaces
Right: 1 space

Notes:
The boundaries of the map are also considered blockers. Need to find all robot positions that satisfy these exact distance requirements in all four directions

Task:
Return the coordinates of all robots that meet these movement constraints.

post.content: I got this mf in interview. gave out bruteforce but interviewer requried dp solution. reached to dp state and partially coded solution but ran out of time. how come leetcode prime does not have this? what\'s point of paying for prime then

Traversing the grid using dp can be done in O(4 * m * n)
DP: O(m * n * 4)
Later, serialize the distance of blockers in all 4 directions in a string, and store the string in map.
then from the queries as well, serialize those, and chk in map. 
The later part takes O(1) roughly.

thanks for sharing the question. 

Were you shortlisted for further rounds?
COuld you also share the LLD round question?

Hey, I was looking at that robot grid problem \u2014 were you using DFS for it?\nIt seems like it just needs exact straight-line distance checks in 4 directions, not full DFS.\nCurious what part tripped you up \u2014 the exact distance condition or boundary handling?
